docs.text: Introduction to Construct 3 Construct 3 is a powerful and user-friendly game development platform that allows users to create games without needing to write traditional code. With its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and a wide range of built-in features, Construct 3 makes game creation accessible to beginners and efficient for experienced developers. This documentation will provide a basic overview to help you get started. For more detailed guides and tutorials, additional resources and links will be provided throughout this document. Let’s start with a few links to help you understand things more easily and quickly. First, if you’re not very familiar with Construct 3, I recommend reading the official documentation. It explains everything in great detail. Construct 3 Docs Javascript in Construct While this game is developed in Construct 3, it does not rely solely on the traditional event system. Instead, many parts of the game are built using Construct 3’s official JavaScript API. This API allows developers to control layouts, objects, behaviors, and systems directly through JavaScript code, providing greater flexibility and deeper control over the game's logic. It’s important to note that while general JavaScript knowledge is helpful, understanding Construct 3's specific scripting API is essential for working with this project. For more information, please refer to the official guide. Javascript in Construct Docs Kingdom Solitaire Welcome to Kingdom Solitaire, a charming blend of classic gameplay and creative design! In this game, you will earn stars by completing exciting solitaire levels. These stars allow you to decorate and customize rooms throughout the royal castle, unlocking new areas and bringing the kingdom back to life. Whether you're a solitaire enthusiast or someone who loves building and decorating, Kingdom Solitaire offers a fun and rewarding experience filled with strategy, creativity, and a touch of royal magic. It will be a simple but explanatory documentation. We will answer any questions that may be asked quickly. Which function does what? createDeck() This function creates a full deck of standard playing cards by combining all suits (Hearts, Diamonds, Clubs, Spades) with all card values (Ace to King). After building the complete deck, it randomizes the order using a simple shuffle to ensure each game session has a unique deck order. This deck is essential for the Solitaire gameplay, as it forms the base of all card interactions. generateDeck(maxValue, suitCount) This function generates a customized deck depending on the game's difficulty. It restricts the card values (e.g., up to 7 for easy) and limits the number of suits (e.g., only 2 suits for easy). After creating this limited deck, it shuffles the cards randomly. This function supports dynamic difficulty scaling by adjusting the deck composition. save() The save() function allows developers to save the current solitaire level layout into a JSON file. It records the position, size, animation, and free status of each card in the scene. It prompts the user to enter a level number and difficulty, then generates a structured JSON object representing the entire level. It ensures that only significant angles are stored and manages overwriting existing levels if necessary. getRandomCardFace() This utility function randomly selects a card face by choosing a random value and a random suit. It's used whenever a new, free card face needs to be assigned dynamically, such as when revealing a hidden card. checkAndRevealFreeCards() This asynchronous function checks all cards on the layout and determines which cards are now "free" (i.e., no cards are blocking them). If a card becomes free, it animates a flip and optionally spawns a money reward sprite on the card. It plays smooth animations during the transition and introduces randomized money bonuses, enhancing gameplay feedback. isFree(card, allCards) This helper function checks whether a specific card is free to play by verifying if no other cards overlap it with a higher Z-index. It's a vital mechanic for ensuring Solitaire rules are enforced: players can only interact with free cards. getCardValueFromAnimation(animationName) This function extracts the card's rank and suit information from its animation name (e.g., "10Hearts" → rank 10, suit Hearts). It helps the logic determine the card's playable status based on its value. startTutorialTimer() This function sets a timer that, after a delay, calls the tutorial check function. It's used to automatically highlight playable cards for the player if they are inactive for a few seconds, helping guide them through the gameplay experience. isPlayableCard() This critical function checks whether a clicked card is playable according to Solitaire rules: It must be adjacent in value to the base card (e.g., 5 and 6, or King and Ace). Special handling exists if the base card is a "Wild" card, making any free card playable. If a card is playable, it updates the game state, destroys the previous base card, moves the clicked card to the base, handles combo bonuses, and manages rewards. unlockRandomCard() When a move is successfully made, this function randomly selects a locked card in the field and unlocks it. It plays a tween animation to visually show the unlocking and updates the card's lock status, progressively making more cards available to the player. animateCard(card, targetX, targetY, options = {}) This asynchronous function animates the movement of a card from its current position to a target (X, Y) location. If the flip option is enabled, the card first shrinks to simulate a flip, updates its animation (e.g., showing its face), and then expands back to normal size. This function uses Construct 3's Tween behavior to create smooth movement and flipping effects, ensuring a polished user experience. manageBaseCards() This function ensures that only one or two cards are present at the base position at a time. It destroys any excess cards stacked at the base and also manages the move history, keeping it trimmed to a maximum of one undoable move. It ensures clean game state maintenance and prevents unwanted clutter on the base card area. getSmartCardAnimation() This function helps determine which card should be drawn next when the player draws a card from the deck. It tries to prioritize generating a playable card if the player is due (after every three draws or by random chance), increasing the chance that the player can continue playing without getting stuck. drawCard() This core gameplay function handles drawing a new card from the deck and placing it onto the base. It manages animations, updates the deck counter, handles newly added hand cards, resets combo streaks when a new card is drawn, and checks for game completion conditions after the card is played. undoMove() This function allows players to undo their last move (with a cost of in-game currency). It restores the previous base card, adjusts the hand cards layout, and refunds the card back to the deck. Undoing provides a critical second-chance mechanic, especially in difficult levels. normalizeAngle(degrees) A utility function that ensures a given angle value stays within the 0–360° range. It helps correct any rotation values that might become negative or exceed a full rotation due to gameplay transformations. initGame() The initGame() function initializes the solitaire level by loading the corresponding JSON level data. It spawns cards at the correct positions, assigns their animations, determines which cards are free or locked, creates the initial hand deck, and sets the base card. It also handles starting the tutorial timer and setting up the level difficulty. dartBooster() This booster mechanic allows players to destroy a random facedown card (not in hand) by paying in-game currency. It's useful for removing problematic cards that block progress. After destroying a card, it checks for newly freed cards and updates the field accordingly. windmillBooster() The windmill booster lets players destroy all free cards on the field at once, massively clearing the board at the cost of a high amount of in-game currency. It plays animations for each card and refreshes the field afterward, giving players a powerful comeback tool. wildBooster() The wild booster changes the current base card into a Wild card after a quick animation. Wild cards allow any adjacent free card to be played next, making it an incredibly valuable strategic advantage for the player. deckBooster() The deck booster allows players to add extra cards to their hand and/or to the remaining deck count. It uses in-game currency and dynamically adjusts how many cards are added to the hand versus to the deck, depending on available space. checkPlayableTutorial() This function helps new players by automatically highlighting playable cards during the game. It checks whether there are any valid moves based on the current base card. If possible moves exist, it triggers animations to visually guide the player. If the base card is a Wild, all free cards become valid. This function is called periodically by the tutorial timer to assist stuck players. checkGameCompletion() This function continuously checks if the game should end. If no field cards remain, it triggers a level completion ("win"). If the deck and hand are empty but field cards remain, it triggers a game failure ("lose"). If no more playable moves exist, it also results in a game failure. The system ensures smooth level progression and failure handling based on Solitaire rules. updateComboSprites(comboCount) This function visually updates the combo indicator sprites based on the current combo streak. If the player plays multiple cards consecutively, combo sprites light up to reflect their progress, motivating players to build higher combos. updateVariable(operation, key, value) This function updates an in-game variable (like "gold", "stars", "lives") in localStorage and inside Construct 3 global variables. Depending on the operation ("add", "sub", "set"), it modifies the value accordingly. It also ensures special conditions, like limiting the number of lives, and refreshes any UI text associated with that variable. updateText(key) This function updates the text values displayed in the game by syncing the corresponding UI objects (spriteText) with the latest values from localStorage. It ensures that resource counters like stars or gold shown to the player are always accurate. datainfo(key) A simple utility function to retrieve any numeric game data (like gold, stars, lives) from localStorage by automatically attaching the game prefix. It returns the value as an integer or 0 if the key does not exist. playRewardAnimations() This function plays queued reward animations that have been stored in localStorage. Each reward (such as receiving gold or cards) triggers a visual animation after a small delay, creating a satisfying reward loop. After playing all animations, the reward queue is cleared. addToA(name, quantity, extraData = {}) This function adds a new reward (e.g., "gold 100") into the animation queue saved in localStorage. It lets the game stack multiple rewards and play them sequentially later, especially after completing collections or challenges. updateCurrentLevelText() This function updates the display text of the start button in menus to show the player's current level. It ensures that players always see the correct level number they are about to play next, based on localStorage data. claimReward(collectionName) This function handles the reward claiming process when a player completes a collection of cards. It checks if all cards are collected, marks the collection as completed, parses the reward (like extra gold), and triggers the reward screen inside Construct 3. dropCard() This function manages random card drops for collections. It randomly selects two cards (giving priority to uncollected ones) and updates the player's collection data in localStorage. It prepares the visual data for displaying dropped cards and adjusts collection progress accordingly. updatePartsVisibility() This function controls the visibility of furniture and room items (called "parts") based on what the player has unlocked. It hides all parts first, then shows only the parts that are unlocked for the currently active room. It's called whenever the room is refreshed or a new item is bought. getCurrentRoom() This function retrieves the player's current room from localStorage (defaulting to Kitchen if missing) and updates the UI accordingly. It also prepares the list of available items to buy, updates progress bars, sets prices, and synchronizes the parts visibility. buyControl(buttonIndex) This function handles purchasing new room decorations. It checks if the player has enough stars to unlock an item, updates the item’s unlocked status, deducts the cost, updates UI elements, and triggers success or failure animations in Construct 3. It keeps track of player progression within the room. nextRoom() This function switches the player to the next available room in the game. It cycles through the list of rooms, saves the new current room into localStorage, and refreshes the room UI so players can continue decorating other parts of the kingdom. nextRoom() This function handles moving the player to the next room in the Room Decoration system. It reads the current room from localStorage, finds its position within the master room list, selects the next room (looping back to the beginning if necessary), updates localStorage, and refreshes the UI to display the newly selected room and its corresponding decorations. showNextLevels() This function manages the unlocking and display of the next set of levels inside a city. It calculates the appropriate starting level for the new set, updates the visibility and animation frames of the level icons (showing whether they are completed, current, or locked), and dynamically adjusts UI elements like level labels and progression indicators. It also creates a special marker (e.g., a plane sprite) on the last unlocked level to guide the player. updateCityText() This function updates the city information displayed in the lobby. Based on the player's current level, it identifies which city they are currently playing in and updates related UI elements like the city title, city background sprite, and associated UI graphics. If the player completes enough levels within a city, the system also dynamically adjusts which levels and rewards are visible. checkGameCompletion() This is one of the core gameplay functions that determines whether the player has won or lost the current level. It checks: If all field cards are cleared (player wins), If the player has no cards in hand or deck and there are still field cards left (player loses), If there are no playable moves available (player loses). Depending on the outcome, it triggers the appropriate Construct 3 event for level completion or failure. updateComboSprites(comboCount) This function visually updates combo progress indicators based on the number of consecutive successful plays the player has made. It adjusts the animation frame of dedicated "combo" sprites to show the player's current combo streak, enhancing feedback and rewarding skilled gameplay. Getting started and some questions I recommend you to change the prefix at the top of the main js file. It is important for localstorage. e.g. const gamePrefix = "Solitaire2_"; > const gamePrefix = "mysolitairegame_"; How do I add gold to a player? Just add js block to events and write this code: updateVariable("add","gold",100) How do I set gold to a player? updateVariable("set","gold",100) How do I sub gold to a player? updateVariable("sub","gold",100) You can also add the star with the same variable. Or anything you want to keep in localstorage. updateVariable("add","star",100) -I'm developing the game and at some point I need to reset it. open a js block again and write the following code. this will reset the game. for example, the levels you passed will be reset. localStorage.clear(); and Restart Layout How can I update the player data that I have set text on any page? updateVariable("add","gold",0); If you add with a value of 0, it will print directly to the text. Support If you have any questions, please contact me: posegraphic@gmail.com
docs.title: Ana Sayfa

docs.text: While this game is developed in Construct 3, it does not rely solely on the traditional event system. Instead, many parts of the game are built using Construct 3’s official JavaScript API. This API allows developers to control layouts, objects, behaviors, and systems directly through JavaScript code, providing greater flexibility and deeper control over the game's logic. It’s important to note that while general JavaScript knowledge is helpful, understanding Construct 3's specific scripting API is essential for working with this project. docs.text: I recommend you to change the prefix at the top of the main js file. It is important for localstorage. e.g. const gamePrefix = "Solitaire2_"; > const gamePrefix = "mysolitairegame_"; How do I add gold to a player? Just add js block to events and write this code: updateVariable("add","gold",100) How do I set gold to a player? updateVariable("set","gold",100) How do I sub gold to a player? updateVariable("sub","gold",100) You can also add the star with the same variable. Or anything you want to keep in localstorage. updateVariable("add","star",100) -I'm developing the game and at some point I need to reset it. open a js block again and write the following code. this will reset the game. for example, the levels you passed will be reset. localStorage.clear(); and Restart Layout How can I update the player data that I have set text on any page? updateVariable("add","gold",0); If you add with a value of 0, it will print directly to the text.
